title: Rivalry, solidarity, and longevity among siblings
abstract: Family composition and household dynamics, both in early and in later life, influence individual health and longevity. Both positive and negative effects can be expected in terms of sibling size and composition. On one hand, siblings compete with each other, which may lead to resource dilution and increased adult mortality risks. On the other hand, siblings protect and care for each other, which may have a positive impact on longevity.
authors_text: Donrovich, Robyn; Puschmann, Paul; Matthijs, Koen
